query: Cycle to the Sun gran fondo cycling Pa'ia

videos.title: cycle to the sun start
videos.description: 200 riders from around the world cross the starting line at the 2012 Cycle to the Sun on August 25, 2012, on Maui. The race began in the sea-level town of Paia and traveled to the 10,000 foot high summit of Haleakala. The first place male  finisher was Takayuki Maganuma of Japan, with a time of 2:54:32. The second place male finisher was Eric Lau of Oahu, with a time of 2:54:58. The first place female finisher was Trina Van Dyck of Alberta, Canada, with a time of 3:26:50. Jennifer Bitner of Olympia, Washington was the second place female finisher with a time of 3:33:05. Additional results can be found at www.cycletothesun.com.

videos.title: Cycle to The Sun
videos.description:
On the island of Maui, 3000 miles west of the US Mainland, there exists a legendary ride which starts in a beachside town called Pa'ia and climbs 36 miles to the summit of Haleakala Volcano, Hawai'i's sacred House of the Sun, at over 10,000 feet altitude. The ride starts a block from hot, windy Baldwin Beach, climbs through the cool, grassy meadows and bronco-busting pastures of an area known as Upcountry, and finishes in chilly temperatures amongst a moonscape of volcanic rock in a section of Haleakala National Park known as Sliding Sands. If the weather at the summit is clear, riders can see 5 other Hawai'ian islands as they finish: Moloka'i, Lana'i, Kaho'olawe, Molokini, and Hawai'i Island.

This video was produced by Grace Delivers Video Specialist Emily Sullivan

nce a year, this ride becomes an individual and collective test against the clock known as Cycle To The Sun. Riders come to Maui from around the world to test their skills at Maui's The House of the Sun. Do you dare to take the challenge?

videos.title: The brutal reality of winter bikepacking
videos.description:
We trekked our bikes across snowy Mount Orjen - we were rewarded with some incredible camp spots and spectacular sun rises and sun sets. 

We hope you enjoy watching - let us know what you think!

Here is a link to the route: https://www.komoot.com/tour/2056107847?share_token=aen46AmHGFdRn8WSbENsIYI4f4rTqxQPDqfX4cAcrGR2KVt31c&ref=wtd
